title: Good vs Pleasant Choice
summary: Yama explains the distinction between Shreya (the good/spiritual path) and Preya (the pleasant/sensual path). Addresses temptation, short-term pleasure vs long-term success, habits, and self-control.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “Two concepts bind the man and are of different objectives. One is what is beneficial and the other is what is pleasing. He who chooses the…” through “O! Nachiketa! Having seen that great and exalted state, the very foundation of the universe, the end-point of all observances, the other shore…” through “Smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest is the Atman that dwells in the cave of the heart of beings. He who is desireless, realises…”

Themes: self-knowledge, mind and meditation, mortality and immortality. The passage moves from “While seated, he travels far ; lying down he goes all-over. Who can know other than me that Shining One, who is both joyful and joyless.” through “By the study of the scriptures, lecturing one's own intellectual enquiries or by wide learning or listening, this Atman cannot be realised.…” through “He for whom the Brahmana and the Kshatriya are food, and Death the pickle, who can know where He (The Self, Atman) is ?”

attribution.rights: Public domain in the United States

verses.sanskrit: अन्यच्छ्रेयोऽन्यदुतैव प्रेय- स्ते उभे नानार्थे पुरुषँ सिनीतः । तयोः श्रेय आददानस्य साधु भवति हीयतेऽर्थाद्य उ प्रेयो वृणीते ॥
verses.translation: Two concepts bind the man and are of different objectives. One is what is beneficial and the other is what is pleasing. He who chooses the beneficial, gains and the one who goes for the the pleasant, deflects from that.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयश्च प्रेयश्च मनुष्यमेतः तौ सम्परीत्य विविनक्ति धीरः । श्रेयो हि धीरोऽभि प्रेयसो वृणीते प्रेयो मन्दो योगक्षेमाद्वृणीते ॥
verses.translation: Both these - beneficial and pleasant - come to humans. The discriminating one after due consideration selects the beneficial alone in preference to the pleasure-giving; but desirous of worldly happiness, the dull-witted falls for pleasing.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं प्रियान्प्रियरूपांश्च कामान् अभिध्यायन्नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः । नैतां सृङ्कां वित्तमयीमवाप्तो यस्यां मज्जन्ति बहवो मनुष्याः ॥
verses.translation: O! Nachiketa!, After due consideration, you have rejected all those pleasures and objects of pleasures, which also tempt. You have not got into this path of pleasures, in which many men are lost.

verses.sanskrit: दूरमेते विपरीते विषूची अविद्या या च विद्येति ज्ञाता । विद्याभीप्सिनं नचिकेतसं मन्ये न त्वा कामा बहवोऽलोलुपन्त ॥
verses.translation: Widely varying with different destinations are these two- ignorance and knowledge. I think Nachiketas is desirous of knowledge as the objects of pleasure do not tempt him.

verses.sanskrit: अविद्यायामन्तरे वर्तमानाः स्वयं धीराः पण्डितंमन्यमानाः । दन्द्रम्यमाणाः परियन्ति मूढा अन्धेनैव नीयमाना यथान्धाः ॥
verses.translation: The deluded staying in illusion thinking themselves to be enlightened go in circles faltering and floundering like sightless-men led by a sightless one.

verses.sanskrit: न साम्परायः प्रतिभाति बालं प्रमाद्यन्तं वित्तमोहेन मूढम् । अयं लोको नास्ति पर इति मानी पुनः पुनर्वशमापद्यते मे ॥
verses.translation: Deluded by riches, one in illusion like a child thinks only of this world and not of a hereafter - and thus, gets under my control repeatedly.

verses.sanskrit: श्रवणायापि बहुभिर्यो न लभ्यः श‍ृण्वन्तोऽपि बहवो यं न विद्युः । आश्चर्यो वक्ता कुशलोऽस्य लब्धा आश्चर्यो ज्ञाता कुशलानुशिष्टः ॥
verses.translation: It is not possible for many to hear about it (Brahman) and even having heard many do not understand. The teacher must be a skilful one and the pupil should be sharp in grasp. The knower is a wonderful person instructed by the adept.

verses.sanskrit: न नरेणावरेण प्रोक्त एष सुविज्ञेयो बहुधा चिन्त्यमानः । अनन्यप्रोक्ते गतिरत्र नास्ति अणीयान् ह्यतर्क्यमणुप्रमाणात् ॥
verses.translation: This principle (Brahman) is not understood by pondering in different ways or being taught by one of low ability. There is no other way than being taught by one of unparalleled ability and it transcends all arguments and is subtlest of subtle.

verses.sanskrit: नैषा तर्केण मतिरापनेया प्रोक्तान्येनैव सुज्ञानाय प्रेष्ठ । यां त्वमापः सत्यधृतिर्बतासि त्वादृङ्नो भूयान्नचिकेतः प्रष्टा ॥
verses.translation: This knowledge is not attainable by argumentation. It is of easy comprehension only when instructed by another. O! Dearest one! O! Nachiketa! let there be seekers like you! You are truly resolved in your desire.

verses.sanskrit: जानाम्यहं शेवधिरित्यनित्यं न ह्यध्रुवैः प्राप्यते हि ध्रुवं तत् । ततो मया नाचिकेतश्चितोऽग्निः अनित्यैर्द्रव्यैः प्राप्तवानस्मि नित्यम् ॥
verses.translation: I am aware that riches are not permanent. Certainly what is everlasting is not attained through what is temporary. But, I have gained the eternal by performing the nachiketa fire-sacrifice with transient materials.

verses.sanskrit: कामस्याप्तिं जगतः प्रतिष्ठां क्रतोरानन्त्यमभयस्य पारम् । स्तोममहदुरुगायं प्रतिष्ठां दृष्ट्वा धृत्या धीरो नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः ॥
verses.translation: O! Nachiketa! Having seen that great and exalted state, the very foundation of the universe, the end-point of all observances, the other shore of fear, You wise one ! You have rejected the attainment of all desires, being steadfast in you resolve.

verses.sanskrit: तं दुर्दर्शं गूढमनुप्रविष्टं गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम् । अध्यात्मयोगाधिगमेन देवं मत्वा धीरो हर्षशोकौ जहाति ॥
verses.translation: The wise man having, by meditating on it , got that which is difficult to realise, effulgent, deep-seated and immanent, dwelling in the cave of the heart transcends both joy and sorrow.

verses.sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा सम्परिगृह्य मर्त्यः प्रवृह्य धर्म्यमणुमेतमाप्य । स मोदते मोदनीयँ हि लब्ध्वा विवृतँ सद्म नचिकेतसं मन्ये ॥
verses.translation: The mortal, having heard and realised that subtle principle, the essence of righteousness, rejoices verily, in a blissful state, having attained the bliss-worthy one. I feel that for Nachiketa , its residence is dis-covered.

verses.sanskrit: अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मा- दन्यत्रास्मात्कृताकृतात् । अन्यत्र भूताच्च भव्याच्च यत्तत्पश्यसि तद्वद ॥
verses.translation: (Nachiketas said): Please tell me that which you perceive as variant from the right and the wrong, the cause and effect and the past and the future.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वे वेदा यत्पदमामनन्ति तपाꣳसि सर्वाणि च यद्वदन्ति । यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति तत्ते पदꣳ सङ्ग्रहेण ब्रवीम्योमित्येतत् ॥
verses.translation: That which is proclaimed by all the scriptures, which is said to be the end effect of all rituals and for which enquiry into the ultimate reality is undertaken, I shall tell you about it briefly as "OM"

verses.sanskrit: एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं ब्रह्म एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं परम् । एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं ज्ञात्वा यो यदिच्छति तस्य तत् ॥
verses.translation: This syllable (OM) is the highest (Brahman) , this alone is ever-lasting and whoever realises this attains whatever he desires.

verses.sanskrit: एतदालम्बनँ श्रेष्ठमेतदालम्बनं परम् । एतदालम्बनं ज्ञात्वा ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
verses.translation: This (syllable OM) is the best support, this is the supreme support and upon realising this, one is adored in the world of Brahma (Hiranyagarbha loka).

verses.sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चि- न्नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित् । अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
verses.translation: The consciousness (soul ) is neither born nor is it subject to death. It has not manifested from anything nor has it given rise to a new entity. This one is unborn, always existing, everlasting, indeterminable in antiquity and is not destroyed when the body (container) is destroyed.

verses.sanskrit: हन्ता चेन्मन्यते हन्तुँ हतश्चेन्मन्यते हतम् । उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायँ हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
verses.translation: If the slayer thinks he slays, and slain thinks that he is slain, neither of them knows the truth. This (Knower) neither slays nor is slain.

verses.sanskrit: अणोरणीयान्महतो महीया- नात्माऽस्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम् । तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ॥
verses.translation: Smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest is the Atman that dwells in the cave of the heart of beings. He who is desireless, realises the glory of Atman through cleansing of mind and senses, and transcends from sorrow.

verses.sanskrit: आसीनो दूरं व्रजति शयानो याति सर्वतः । कस्तं मदामदं देवं मदन्यो ज्ञातुमर्हति ॥
verses.translation: While seated, he travels far ; lying down he goes all-over. Who can know other than me that Shining One, who is both joyful and joyless.

verses.sanskrit: अशरीरँ शरीरेष्वनवस्थेष्ववस्थितम् । महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
verses.translation: Having realised the all-pervading Supreme Atman, who is bodiless within the bodies, stable amidst the unstable, the wise one grieves no more.

verses.sanskrit: नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन । यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यः तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूꣳ स्वाम् ॥
verses.translation: By the study of the scriptures, lecturing one's own intellectual enquiries or by wide learning or listening, this Atman cannot be realised. Whomsoever, It chooses alone, can attain that realisation about It, as It reveals Its own nature to him.

verses.sanskrit: नाविरतो दुश्चरितान्नाशान्तो नासमाहितः । नाशान्तमानसो वाऽपि प्रज्ञानेनैनमाप्नुयात् ॥
verses.translation: He who is not free from evil conduct, whose senses are not controlled , whose emotions are not harmonised, whose mind is not at peace, cannot attain this Atman by any means of knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य ब्रह्म च क्षत्रं च उभे भवत ओदनः । मृत्युर्यस्योपसेचनं क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ॥
verses.translation: He for whom the Brahmana and the Kshatriya are food, and Death the pickle, who can know where He (The Self, Atman) is ?
